title: Slab coaster set with pressed texture
subtitle: Six air-dry clay coasters with varied impressed surface patterns
excerpt: A set of six square coasters cut from rolled air-dry clay slabs and impressed with different surface textures using found objects, stamps, or lace. Sealed with varnish after drying. No kiln or wheel required.
sourceNotes: Slab coaster with impressed texture technique derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pattern created by pressing a textured object or tool into soft clay to leave an imprint in the surface.
A flat, even sheet of clay produced by rolling.

content.text: Slab coasters are one of the most accessible air-dry clay projects: roll, impress, cut, dry, seal. The interest comes from the texture. A set of six works well with each coaster having a different pattern, using different objects to create the
: a button, a piece of lace, a leaf, a carved stamp, a fork, or a textured fabric scrap.

items.name: Texture objects: buttons, lace, leaves, stamps, forks

content.text: 6-8 mm thick. Use two wooden guide strips of equal thickness on either side of the clay to ensure an even thickness. Cut six squares using the card template and fettling knife. A 90 x 90 mm square with 8 mm thickness is a practical coaster size.

Press your chosen texture object firmly onto the surface of each cut square before moving them to dry. Press straight down and lift straight up to avoid smearing. For lace or fabric, roll gently over the surface with the rolling pin after pressing by hand. For leaf impressions, lay the leaf vein-side down and roll across it once, then peel away. Each square should have a different texture for visual variety in the set.

Allow to dry flat on the board for 24-48 hours. Lift each coaster once after the first 12 hours and place on a dry surface to dry the underside. Once bone-dry, sand the edges and corners lightly with fine sandpaper to remove any sharp cut marks. Apply two coats of acrylic sealant or diluted PVA (50:50) on all surfaces including the base. The sealant prevents moisture absorption; air-dry clay coasters without sealant will absorb condensation from a cold glass and soften.
Cut round coasters using a 90 mm circle cutter or a glass as a template.
Paint the recessed texture areas with a contrasting acrylic wash before sealing to make the pattern stand out.
Press a small cork tile onto the underside of each coaster with PVA after sealing to protect furniture surfaces.

rows.problem: Coasters curl upward at the corners during drying
rows.cause: Drying too fast or the slab was too thin at the edges
rows.fix: Weight the corners with small smooth stones for the first 12 hours of drying, or dry between two boards with a light weight on top.

rows.cause: Object was not pressed firmly enough, or clay was too stiff
rows.fix: Ensure the clay is soft and fresh before impressing. Apply firm, even pressure over the whole object surface.

rows.problem: Sealant looks glossy rather than matte
rows.fix: Sand lightly once dry and apply a matte or satin sealant on the next coat.
